summary: Machine policy for Windows Error Reporting. Microsoft documents the same device policy path and value name that the app writes, and the policy description matches the disabled behavior.

setting.name: Windows Error Reporting
setting.casual_explanation: Windows Error Reporting can send information about crashes and failures so Microsoft or an internal server can analyze them. This policy turns that reporting off for the device.
setting.why_people_change_it: People turn it off when they do not want crash and failure reports sent out of the device or when they want a stricter privacy baseline.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off blocks problem reports and removes solution information in Security and Maintenance. Leaving it not configured means this specific policy does not force reporting off, and other reporting settings can still decide behavior.
setting.professional_notes: ErrorReporting.admx and the ADMX_ErrorReporting Policy CSP map Disabled to HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Error Reporting\Disabled with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. Microsoft's local ICM policy text also notes that the broader Internet Communication Management policy can take precedence when this specific setting is disabled or not configured.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: This specific policy does not force Windows Error Reporting off. The broader ICM policy or Control Panel settings can still decide behavior.

allowed_values.label: Windows Error Reporting disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Problem information is not sent through Windows Error Reporting, and solution information is not available in Security and Maintenance.

allowed_values.label: Policy not forcing Windows Error Reporting off
allowed_values.meaning: This specific disable policy is turned off, so the broader ICM policy or Control Panel settings can decide Windows Error Reporting behavior.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes Disabled = 1 under the machine Windows Error Reporting policy key, which matches the documented turn-off state.

windows_defaults.label: Windows device-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows Vista and newer systems exposing the Windows Error Reporting policy

states.rationale: The machine disable policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: When this specific policy is not configured, broader Internet Communication Management policy or Control Panel settings can still govern Windows Error Reporting.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Systems that want Windows Error Reporting left under ordinary system policy
avoid_for: Privacy baselines that want reporting explicitly disabled

states.rationale: Leave the machine disable policy unset.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows Error Reporting behavior left to the normal policy and user-setting flow.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You explicitly want crash and failure reporting blocked.
tradeoffs: Windows Error Reporting can still operate depending on other policy and user settings.
No hard device-wide reporting block is enforced by this value.

recommended_profiles.label: No error reporting
intended_for: Managed desktops that should not send crash reports out
avoid_for: People who want Microsoft or enterprise crash analysis and solution lookups

states.rationale: Turn off Windows Error Reporting through machine policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want a clear device-wide policy that blocks Windows Error Reporting.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want crash reporting and solution information left available.
tradeoffs: Crash and failure reports are not sent through Windows Error Reporting.
Solution information in Security and Maintenance is not available.

evidence.title: Microsoft ADMX_ErrorReporting Policy CSP: WerDisable_2
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the machine policy that turns Windows Error Reporting off, including the registry mapping.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft ErrorReporting.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps the machine policy to SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Error Reporting\Disabled with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft ErrorReporting.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy stops problem information from being sent and removes solution information, while disabling or not configuring it defers to broader policy precedence.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft ICM.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ICM help text documents the broader 'Turn off Windows Error Reporting' policy and confirms that it overrides Control Panel settings.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes Disabled = 1 under the machine Windows Error Reporting policy key.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: Microsoft documents WerDisable_2 directly in the ADMX_ErrorReporting Policy CSP and maps it to Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Error Reporting\Disabled. The same page describes that enabling the policy prevents problem information from being sent and removes solution information, and the app writes that exact machine policy surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: If you enable this policy setting, Windows Error Reporting is disabled. This setting prevents problem information from being sent to Microsoft. In addition, Solution information is not available in Security and Maintenance in Control Panel. ADMX mapping: Registry Key Name Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Error Reporting; Registry Value Name Disabled.
validation_proof.notes: The ADMX_ErrorReporting Policy CSP page explicitly names the exact machine policy path and value name the app writes, and its policy description matches the disabled behavior.
